pattern: Count fuel-bounded walks with a memoized recursion keyed on (current city, fuel remaining), crediting a route every time the walk is standing at the destination, whether or not it keeps going.
hints: The destination can be visited more than once in a single valid route (you may pass through and keep driving), so count it as a hit every time you're there rather than stopping the recursion.
Since order and starting city matter but only remaining fuel constrains future moves, memoize on (current city, fuel left) — with up to 100 cities and 200 fuel that state space is tiny.
From each state, add 1 if you're at the destination, then for every other city whose distance costs no more than the fuel left, recurse into (that city, fuel left - cost) and sum, all modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: start equals finish and fuel is too small to leave and come back, so only the trivial route counts
just two cities with fuel that permits several back-and-forth trips
three cities where reaching the destination directly costs more fuel than a detour through the middle city
fuel exactly equal to the single direct hop distance
the destination is unreachable from the start given the fuel budget
start and finish at opposite ends of a four-city line
two intermediate cities sit at equal distance from the start, forcing the recursion to branch through either

stress.description: up to 100 distinct cities at random positions with the fuel budget maxed out
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(100, max(2, size))
    locations = rng.sample(range(0, 100000), n)
    start = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    finish = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    fuel = 200
    return [locations, start, finish, fuel]
stress.note: distinct random positions with max fuel exercises the full (position, fuel) memo table
